title: Gmail Phishing Report Analysis
dataSources: Email log (`PHISHING_REPORT`, user report button events in audit)
spl:
index=gws sourcetype="gws:gmail" event_name="PHISHING_REPORT"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by reporter, _time
| where count > 5
| sort -count
description: User-reported phishing provides ground-truth for tuning secure links and awareness; volume spikes indicate campaigns.
value: User-reported phishing provides ground-truth for tuning secure links and awareness; volume spikes indicate campaigns.
implementation: Ingest reported-message metadata (no body in Splunk if policy requires). Correlate with Postini/Workspace security dashboards. Feed SOC for IOC extraction.
visualization: Line chart (reports per hour), Table (top reporters), Bar chart (campaign hash if extracted).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Email log (`PHISHING_REPORT`, user report button events in audit).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest reported-message metadata (no body in Splunk if policy requires). Correlate with Postini/Workspace security dashboards. Feed SOC for IOC extraction.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:gmail" event_name="PHISHING_REPORT"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by reporter, _time
| where count > 5
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Gmail Phishing Report Analysis** ‚Äî User-reported phishing provides ground-truth for tuning secure links and awareness; volume spikes indicate campaigns.

Documented **Data sources**: Email log (`PHISHING_REPORT`, user report button events in audit). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gws; **sourcetype**: gws:gmail. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=gws, sourcetype="gws:gmail". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by reporter, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where count > 5` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with Google Admin console audit and Google Workspace reports for the same org unit and time window.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (reports per hour), Table (top reporters), Bar chart (campaign hash if extracted).
grandmaExplanation: We use this to stay ahead of email and collaboration problems, so the team is not the last to know when something drifts or breaks.
knownFalsePositives: User training exercises and simulated phish that spike reported-message volume.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
